content.text:
Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

content.text: I'll analyze the codebase to create a useful CLAUDE.md. Let me explore the project structure and key files.

input.prompt:
Thoroughly explore this codebase to understand its architecture, build system, testing, and development workflow. I need to create a CLAUDE.md file.

Please investigate:
1. Project structure (top-level directories and their purpose)
2. Build/run/test commands (look at Makefile, package.json, pyproject.toml, setup.py, docker files, etc.)
3. Architecture - how the different parts connect (custom_components, addon, apps directories)
4. Testing setup and how to run tests
5. Any existing configuration files (.cursorrules, .cursor/rules/, .github/copilot-instructions.md, README.md)
6. The tech stack (Python, JavaScript, Home Assistant integration, etc.)
7. How the service is deployed (Docker, Home Assistant addon, etc.)
8. Any linting or formatting tools configured

Be very thorough - check all config files, dockerfiles, manifest files, and key source files to understand the full picture.

content.content:
1→# Hass Flatmate
     2→
     3→Home Assistant-native flat-sharing toolkit with two parts:
     4→
     5→- `custom_components/hass_flatmate`: HACS custom integration
     6→- `apps/hass_flatmate_service`: Home Assistant App (formerly add-on) backend service
     7→
     8→It replaces the flat-sharing features you use with:
     9→
    10→- Shopping list actions (add, complete, delete)
    11→- Fairness distribution (90-day) + SVG chart image
    12→- Weekly cleaning rotation with temporary swaps
    13→- Takeover completion with automatic compensation override
    14→- Home Assistant calendar activity events
    15→- Built-in notification scheduling (Monday + Sunday reminders)
    16→- Optional shopping-added push notifications
    17→- Notification deep links (iOS + Android)
    18→- Automation-friendly activity events on HA event bus
    19→- Safe notification test mode (redirect all notifications to one selected user)
    20→
    21→## One-Click Install
    22→
    23→### Home Assistant App (backend)
    24→
    25→[![Add app repository to your Home Assistant instance.](https://my.home-assistant.io/badges/supervisor_add_addon_repository.svg)](https://my.home-assistant.io/redirect/supervisor_add_addon_repository/?repository_url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fgitviola%2Fhass-flatmate)
    26→[![Show app on your Home Assistant instance.](https://my.home-assistant.io/badges/supervisor_addon.svg)](https://my.home-assistant.io/redirect/supervisor_addon/?addon=hass_flatmate_service&repository_url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fgitviola%2Fhass-flatmate)
    27→
    28→### HACS integration
    29→
    30→[![Open HACS repository on your Home Assistant instance.](https://my.home-assistant.io/badges/hacs_repository.svg)](https://my.home-assistant.io/redirect/hacs_repository/?owner=gitviola&repository=hass-flatmate&category=integration)
    31→
    32→## Repository Layout
    33→
    34→- `/custom_components/hass_flatmate`: install via HACS (custom repository)
    35→- `/apps/hass_flatmate_service`: install via Home Assistant App repository
    36→- `/addon/hass_flatmate_service`: canonical backend source + tests
    37→- `/apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src`: app build source mirror (synced from `addon/`)
    38→
    39→## Quick Start
    40→
    41→1. Install app from this repository (`apps/hass_flatmate_service`) in Home Assistant.
    42→2. Set app option `api_token`.
    43→   - Persistent DB path: `/config/hass_flatmate_service/hass_flatmate.db` (Supervisor `addon_config` mount).
    44→   - Data survives container restarts/image updates; include app/add-on data in HA backups for disaster recovery.
    45→3. Install custom integration via HACS from this repository.
    46→4. Configure integration with:
    47→   - `base_url`: service URL (default `http://ebc95cb1-hass-flatmate-service:8099`)
    48→   - `api_token`: same value from app config
    49→5. In Lovelace storage mode, shopping and cleaning card resources are auto-registered with cache-busting version query params.
    50→6. Add cards/entities from `examples/lovelace-flatmate-dashboard.yaml` (Sections dashboard format).
    51→
    52→## Shopping UI Card
    53→
    54→Use the custom card in any dashboard:
    55→
    56→```yaml
    57→type: custom:hass-flatmate-shopping-card
    58→entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_shopping_data
    59→title: Shared Shopping
    60→```
    61→
    62→You can also add it from the dashboard UI card picker (no manual YAML required).
    63→
    64→Features:
    65→- Add shopping items fast
    66→- Mark item done / delete item
    67→- Quick add from favorites and recents
    68→- Add/remove favorites
    69→
    70→The card uses integration services, so actor attribution stays correct.
    71→
    72→## Shopping Compact UI Card
    73→
    74→Use the dedicated read-only compact card for e-ink/non-touch displays:
    75→
    76→```yaml
    77→type: custom:hass-flatmate-shopping-compact-card
    78→entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_shopping_data
    79→title: Shopping List
    80→```
    81→
    82→Features:
    83→- Dense, bullet-free list layout
    84→- Relative age labels like `added 5 hours ago` / `added 2 weeks ago`
    85→- Read-only rendering optimized for passive displays
    86→- Available in the card picker (no manual YAML required)
    87→
    88→## Cleaning UI Card
    89→
    90→Use the dedicated cleaning card in any dashboard:
    91→
    92→```yaml
    93→type: custom:hass-flatmate-cleaning-card
    94→entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_cleaning_schedule
    95→title: Weekly Cleaning
    96→weeks: 5
    97→layout: interactive
    98→```
    99→
   100→Features:
   101→- Human-friendly week list (`This week`, `Next week`, etc.)
   102→- Clear done/pending/missed status
   103→- Strike-through visual once completed
   104→- Mark-done action in-card
   105→- Non-assignee completion modal (confirm for assignee vs takeover)
   106→- Click any week row (interactive layout) to open 7-day shift history and notification delivery status
   107→- Optional `layout: compact` read-only mode for e-ink/non-touch dashboards
   108→- Compact mode still shows swap/compensation annotations and who is assigned
   109→
   110→Manual swap override remains available via service:
   111→- `hass_flatmate.hass_flatmate_swap_cleaning_week`
   112→
   113→In Lovelace YAML resource mode, add both resources manually as `module` (recommended with `?v=<integration_version>`):
   114→- `/hass_flatmate/static/hass-flatmate-shopping-card.js?v=<integration_version>`
   115→- `/hass_flatmate/static/hass-flatmate-shopping-compact-card.js?v=<integration_version>`
   116→- `/hass_flatmate/static/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js?v=<integration_version>`
   117→
   118→Compact e-ink example:
   119→
   120→```yaml
   121→type: custom:hass-flatmate-cleaning-card
   122→entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_cleaning_schedule
   123→title: Cleaning (E-Ink)
   124→weeks: 5
   125→layout: compact
   126→```
   127→
   128→## Distribution UI Card
   129→
   130→Use the dedicated distribution card instead of the SVG image entity card:
   131→
   132→```yaml
   133→type: custom:hass-flatmate-distribution-card
   134→entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_shopping_distribution_90d
   135→title: Shopping Distribution (90d)
   136→layout: bars
   137→```
   138→
   139→Features:
   140→- Stable native rendering in two styles:
   141→  - `layout: bars` (default)
   142→  - `layout: compact` (single-row boxes, e-ink friendly)
   143→- Shows all flatmates from distribution payload (including zero-count members)
   144→- Uses purchase wording (`N purchases`) and last-90-days context text
   145→
   146→In Lovelace YAML resource mode, also add:
   147→- `/hass_flatmate/static/hass-flatmate-distribution-card.js?v=<integration_version>`
   148→
   149→Compact example:
   150→
   151→```yaml
   152→type: custom:hass-flatmate-distribution-card
   153→entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_shopping_distribution_90d
   154→title: Shopping Distribution (Compact)
   155→layout: compact
   156→```
   157→
   158→## Notification Test Mode
   159→
   160→Use these entities to test all flows without notifying everyone:
   161→- `switch.hass_flatmate_notification_test_mode`
   162→- `select.hass_flatmate_notification_test_target`
   163→- `switch.hass_flatmate_notify_shopping_item_added`
   164→- `text.hass_flatmate_shopping_notification_link`
   165→- `text.hass_flatmate_cleaning_notification_link`
   166→- `select.hass_flatmate_shopping_calendar_target`
   167→- `select.hass_flatmate_cleaning_calendar_target`
   168→
   169→When test mode is enabled, all notifications are redirected to the selected target and prefixed with `[TEST]`.
   170→
   171→`*_notification_link` accepts:
   172→- Relative dashboard/view path like `/dashboard-flatmate/shopping`
   173→- Full URL like `https://...`
   174→- Companion deep link like `homeassistant://navigate/dashboard-flatmate/shopping`
   175→
   176→The integration attaches this link to mobile notifications using both iOS `url` and Android `clickAction`.
   177→
   178→## Automation Event Triggers
   179→
   180→The integration emits Home Assistant bus events for every new activity row:
   181→- Generic: `hass_flatmate_activity`
   182→- Action-specific: `hass_flatmate_activity_<action>`
   183→  - Example: `hass_flatmate_activity_shopping_item_added`
   184→  - Example: `hass_flatmate_activity_cleaning_done`
   185→
   186→Event payload includes:
   187→- `activity_id`, `domain`, `action`, `created_at`
   188→- `actor_member_id`, `actor_name`, `actor_user_id_raw`
   189→- `payload` (raw activity payload JSON), `summary`
   190→
   191→This enables per-user custom automations/notifications without changing integration code.
   192→
   193→## Manual Data Import
   194→
   195→Use service `hass_flatmate_import_manual_data` from Developer Tools to paste CSV-like rows:
   196→
   197→- `rotation_rows` format:
   198→  - `date,member_name`
   199→  - date can be any date inside that week (`YYYY-MM-DD` or ISO datetime)
   200→  - rows can be newline or `;` separated
   201→- `cleaning_history_rows` format:
   202→  - `date,member_name[,status][,completed_by_name]`
   203→  - status: `done` (default), `missed`, `pending`
   204→- `shopping_history_rows` format:
   205→  - `date,item_name,buyer_name`
   206→- `cleaning_override_rows` format:
   207→  - `date,member_from_name,member_to_name[,override_type]`
   208→  - `override_type`: `compensation` (default) or `manual_swap`
   209→  - For compensation, `member_from_name` is the person whose baseline turn is being covered.
   210→
   211→Example:
   212→
   213→```yaml
   214→service: hass_flatmate.hass_flatmate_import_manual_data
   215→data:
   216→  rotation_rows: |
   217→    2026-02-14,Martin
   218→    2026-02-21,Gianmarco
   219→    2026-02-28,María
   220→  cleaning_history_rows: |
   221→    2026-02-01,Martin,done
   222→    2026-02-08,Gianmarco,done
   223→  shopping_history_rows: |
   224→    2026-01-30,Milk,Martin
   225→    2026-01-31,Toilet Paper,Gianmarco
   226→  cleaning_override_rows: |
   227→    2026-03-07,Martin,Martina,compensation
   228→```
   229→
   230→Notes:
   231→- Dates can be any day in the target week. Import normalizes to Monday-week internally.
   232→- Member names must match active hass-flatmate display names exactly (including accents).
   233→
   234→## Automated Tests
   235→
   236→Backend tests run locally without Home Assistant:
   237→
   238→```bash
   239→cd addon/hass_flatmate_service
   240→python3 -m venv .venv
   241→source .venv/bin/activate
   242→pip install -e '.[test]'
   243→pytest
   244→```
   245→
   246→CI also runs on every push/PR:
   247→- Backend test suite
   248→- Integration compile check
   249→- Shopping/cleaning custom card JavaScript syntax check
   250→- Home Assistant app test build (via `home-assistant/builder`)
   251→- Sync guard for `apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src`
   252→
   253→## Image Publishing (GHCR)
   254→
   255→App images are published automatically to GHCR by GitHub Actions:
   256→- Workflow: `.github/workflows/publish-app.yml`
   257→- Trigger: git tag push like `v0.1.1` (also supports Release `published` and manual `workflow_dispatch`)
   258→- Registry image: `ghcr.io/gitviola/hass-flatmate-service-{arch}`
   259→- Architectures: `amd64`, `aarch64`
   260→
   261→Release/tag version must match `apps/hass_flatmate_service/config.yaml` `version`.
   262→
   263→## Changelogs
   264→
   265→- Repo changelog: `/CHANGELOG.md`
   266→- Integration changelog: `/custom_components/hass_flatmate/CHANGELOG.md`
   267→- App changelog: `/apps/hass_flatmate_service/CHANGELOG.md`
   268→
   269→## Syncing App Build Source
   270→
   271→When backend code changes in `addon/hass_flatmate_service`, sync mirror source used by app Docker build:
   272→
   273→```bash
   274→./scripts/sync_app_service_src.sh
   275→```
   276→

content.content:
1→# Architecture
     2→
     3→## Components
     4→
     5→1. Home Assistant App (`apps/hass_flatmate_service`)
     6→- Runs FastAPI + SQLite service on port `8099`.
     7→- Stores all domain state.
     8→- Published for `amd64` and `aarch64` only.
     9→
    10→2. Custom Integration (`custom_components/hass_flatmate`)
    11→- Exposes HA services/entities/image/calendar.
    12→- Syncs HA users/persons into service members.
    13→- Dispatches notifications through HA notify services.
    14→- Hosts static frontend asset for custom shopping card.
    15→- Provides notification test mode switch/select to redirect notifications safely.
    16→
    17→## Data Ownership
    18→
    19→Service owns:
    20→- members
    21→- shopping_items
    22→- shopping_favorites
    23→- activity_events
    24→- rotation_config
    25→- cleaning_assignments
    26→- cleaning_overrides
    27→
    28→Integration owns:
    29→- polling coordinator cache
    30→- HA entity representations
    31→- HA service endpoints and call context mapping
    32→
    33→## Notification Flow
    34→
    35→1. Integration triggers due-check every minute.
    36→2. Service returns notifications due for the exact minute.
    37→3. Integration dispatches returned payloads via `notify.*` services.
    38→
    39→## Calendar Flow
    40→
    41→1. Service stores completion activity events.
    42→2. Integration calendar entity maps activity events to 15-minute calendar entries.
    43→

content.content:
1→# Testing
     2→
     3→## Local backend tests
     4→
     5→```bash
     6→cd addon/hass_flatmate_service
     7→python3 -m venv .venv
     8→source .venv/bin/activate
     9→pip install -e '.[test]'
    10→pytest
    11→```
    12→
    13→Current suite covers:
    14→- Shopping lifecycle + stats + unknown actor exclusion
    15→- Recents/favorites behavior
    16→- SVG rendering endpoint
    17→- Cleaning rotation, manual swaps, takeover completion
    18→- Compensation override auto-planning and conflict handling
    19→- Monday/Sunday notification schedule behavior
    20→
    21→CI additionally runs a Home Assistant app test build using `home-assistant/builder@2025.11.0` for `amd64` and `aarch64`.
    22→CI also validates custom shopping card JavaScript syntax.
    23→
    24→## Fast feedback workflow
    25→
    26→1. Implement/change backend code in `addon/hass_flatmate_service`.
    27→2. Run local tests.
    28→3. Sync app build mirror:
    29→   - `./scripts/sync_app_service_src.sh`
    30→4. Build/deploy app in Home Assistant only after tests pass.
    31→

content.content:
1→[project]
     2→name = "hass-flatmate-service"
     3→version = "0.1.20"
     4→description = "Flatmate shopping and cleaning service for Home Assistant"
     5→requires-python = ">=3.12"
     6→dependencies = [
     7→  "fastapi>=0.115.0,<1.0.0",
     8→  "uvicorn>=0.30.0,<1.0.0",
     9→  "sqlalchemy>=2.0.30,<3.0.0",
    10→  "pydantic>=2.8.0,<3.0.0",
    11→]
    12→
    13→[project.optional-dependencies]
    14→test = [
    15→  "pytest>=8.0.0,<9.0.0",
    16→  "httpx>=0.27.0,<1.0.0",
    17→]
    18→
    19→[build-system]
    20→requires = ["setuptools>=68", "wheel"]
    21→build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
    22→
    23→[tool.pytest.ini_options]
    24→addopts = "-q"
    25→testpaths = ["tests"]
    26→

content.content:
1→name: CI
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→  pull_request:
     6→
     7→permissions:
     8→  contents: read
     9→
    10→jobs:
    11→  backend-tests:
    12→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    13→    steps:
    14→      - name: Checkout
    15→        uses: actions/checkout@v6
    16→
    17→      - name: Setup Python
    18→        uses: actions/setup-python@v6
    19→        with:
    20→          python-version: "3.12"
    21→
    22→      - name: Install backend test deps
    23→        run: |
    24→          cd addon/hass_flatmate_service
    25→          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
    26→          pip install -e '.[test]'
    27→
    28→      - name: Run backend tests
    29→        run: |
    30→          cd addon/hass_flatmate_service
    31→          pytest
    32→
    33→      - name: Compile custom integration
    34→        run: |
    35→          python -m compileall custom_components/hass_flatmate
    36→
    37→  sync-app-source-check:
    38→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    39→    steps:
    40→      - name: Checkout
    41→        uses: actions/checkout@v6
    42→
    43→      - name: Validate app source mirror sync
    44→        run: |
    45→          ./scripts/sync_app_service_src.sh
    46→          git diff --exit-code -- apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src
    47→
    48→  frontend-card-check:
    49→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    50→    steps:
    51→      - name: Checkout
    52→        uses: actions/checkout@v6
    53→
    54→      - name: Setup Node
    55→        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
    56→        with:
    57→          node-version: "22"
    58→
    59→      - name: Validate shopping and cleaning card syntax
    60→        run: |
    61→          node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-shopping-card.js
    62→          node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js
    63→          node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-distribution-card.js
    64→
    65→  app-build-test:
    66→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    67→    steps:
    68→      - name: Checkout
    69→        uses: actions/checkout@v6
    70→
    71→      - name: Test Home Assistant app build
    72→        uses: home-assistant/builder@2025.11.0
    73→        with:
    74→          args: |
    75→            --test \
    76→            --amd64 \
    77→            --aarch64 \
    78→            --target ./apps/hass_flatmate_service \
    79→            --image ghcr.io/gitviola/hass-flatmate-service-{arch}
    80→

content.content:
1→name: Publish App Images
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  release:
     5→    types: [published]
     6→  push:
     7→    tags:
     8→      - "v*"
     9→  workflow_dispatch:
    10→    inputs:
    11→      version:
    12→        description: "Version to publish (e.g. 0.1.0 or v0.1.0)"
    13→        required: true
    14→        type: string
    15→
    16→permissions:
    17→  contents: read
    18→  packages: write
    19→
    20→jobs:
    21→  publish:
    22→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    23→    steps:
    24→      - name: Checkout
    25→        uses: actions/checkout@v6
    26→
    27→      - name: Resolve version
    28→        id: version
    29→        shell: bash
    30→        run: |
    31→          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "release" ]; then
    32→            raw="${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
    33→          elif [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "push" ]; then
    34→            raw="${{ github.ref_name }}"
    35→          else
    36→            raw="${{ inputs.version }}"
    37→          fi
    38→          version="${raw#v}"
    39→          echo "version=${version}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
    40→
    41→      - name: Validate config version
    42→        shell: bash
    43→        run: |
    44→          config_version="$(sed -n 's/^version:[[:space:]]*"\{0,1\}\([^"]*\)"\{0,1\}$/\1/p' apps/hass_flatmate_service/config.yaml)"
    45→          if [ -z "$config_version" ]; then
    46→            echo "Unable to read apps/hass_flatmate_service/config.yaml version"
    47→            exit 1
    48→          fi
    49→          if [ "$config_version" != "${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}" ]; then
    50→            echo "Release version mismatch: config=$config_version, requested=${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
    51→            exit 1
    52→          fi
    53→
    54→      - name: Login to GitHub Container Registry
    55→        uses: docker/login-action@v3
    56→        with:
    57→          registry: ghcr.io
    58→          username: ${{ github.actor }}
    59→          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    60→
    61→      - name: Publish Home Assistant app images
    62→        uses: home-assistant/builder@2025.11.0
    63→        with:
    64→          args: |
    65→            --amd64 \
    66→            --aarch64 \
    67→            --target ./apps/hass_flatmate_service \
    68→            --image hass-flatmate-service-{arch} \
    69→            --docker-hub ghcr.io/gitviola \
    70→            --version ${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
    71→
    72→      - name: Attempt to set package visibility public
    73→        continue-on-error: true
    74→        env:
    75→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    76→        run: |
    77→          for pkg in \
    78→            hass-flatmate-service-amd64 \
    79→            hass-flatmate-service-aarch64 \
    80→            ghcr.io/gitviola/hass-flatmate-service-amd64 \
    81→            ghcr.io/gitviola/hass-flatmate-service-aarch64 \
    82→            ghcr.io/gitviola/ghcr.io/gitviola/hass-flatmate-service-amd64 \
    83→            ghcr.io/gitviola/ghcr.io/gitviola/hass-flatmate-service-aarch64
    84→          do
    85→            encoded_pkg="$(printf '%s' "$pkg" | jq -sRr @uri)"
    86→            gh api --method PATCH \
    87→              -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
    88→              "/user/packages/container/${encoded_pkg}/visibility" \
    89→              -f visibility=public || true
    90→          done
    91→
    92→      - name: Verify anonymous pull works for both architectures
    93→        shell: bash
    94→        run: |
    95→          set -euo pipefail
    96→          version="${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}"
    97→          for arch in amd64 aarch64; do
    98→            image="ghcr.io/gitviola/hass-flatmate-service-${arch}"
    99→            repo="${image#ghcr.io/}"
   100→            for attempt in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do
   101→              token="$(curl -sS "https://ghcr.io/token?service=ghcr.io&scope=repository:${repo}:pull" | jq -r '.token')"
   102→              if [ -n "$token" ] && [ "$token" != "null" ]; then
   103→                status="$(curl -sS -o /tmp/manifest.json -w '%{http_code}' \
   104→                  -H "Authorization: Bearer ${token}" \
   105→                  -H 'Accept: application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json, application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json' \
   106→                  "https://ghcr.io/v2/${repo}/manifests/${version}")"
   107→                if [ "$status" = "200" ]; then
   108→                  break
   109→                fi
   110→              fi
   111→              if [ "$attempt" = "8" ]; then
   112→                echo "Anonymous pull check failed for ${image}:${version} after retries"
   113→                cat /tmp/manifest.json || true
   114→                exit 1
   115→              fi
   116→              sleep 15
   117→            done
   118→          done
   119→

     2→FROM ${BUILD_FROM}
     3→

     5→
     6→LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/gitviola/hass-flatmate"

    19→
    20→CMD ["/run.sh"]

1→"""FastAPI entrypoint for hass-flatmate add-on service."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
     6→from datetime import datetime
     7→
     8→from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, Header, HTTPException, Query, Response, status
     9→from fastapi.responses import PlainTextResponse
    10→from sqlalchemy import select
    11→from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
    12→
    13→from . import db
    14→from .db import Base, get_session
    15→from .models import Member
    16→from .schemas import (
    17→    BuyStatsResponse,
    18→    CleaningCurrentResponse,
    19→    CleaningMarkDoneRequest,
    20→    CleaningMarkUndoneRequest,
    21→    CleaningMarkTakeoverDoneRequest,
    22→    CleaningNotificationDispatchRequest,
    23→    CleaningNotificationDueResponse,
    24→    CleaningScheduleResponse,
    25→    CleaningSwapRequest,
    26→    ManualImportRequest,
    27→    ManualImportResponse,
    28→    FavoritesResponse,
    29→    MemberResponse,
    30→    MembersSyncResponse,
    31→    MembersSyncRequest,
    32→    OperationResponse,
    33→    RecentsResponse,
    34→    ShoppingFavoriteCreateRequest,
    35→    ShoppingItemActionRequest,
    36→    ShoppingItemCreateRequest,
    37→    ShoppingItemResponse,
    38→)
    39→from .services import cleaning, importer, shopping
    40→from .services.activity import list_events
    41→from .services.members import sync_members
    42→from .settings import settings
    43→
    44→
    45→@asynccontextmanager
    46→async def lifespan(_app: FastAPI):
    47→    db.configure_engine()
    48→    db.ensure_db_dir()
    49→    assert db.engine is not None
    50→    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=db.engine)
    51→    yield
    52→
    53→
    54→app = FastAPI(title="hass-flatmate-service", version="0.1.21", lifespan=lifespan)
    55→
    56→
    57→def require_token(x_flatmate_token: str | None = Header(default=None)) -> None:
    58→    if x_flatmate_token != settings.api_token:
    59→        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="Unauthorized")
    60→
    61→
    62→@app.get("/health")
    63→def health() -> dict:
    64→    return {"ok": True}
    65→
    66→
    67→@app.get("/v1/members", response_model=list[MemberResponse], dependencies=[Depends(require_token)])
    68→def get_members(session: Session = Depends(get_session)) -> list[MemberResponse]:
    69→    rows = session.execute(select(Member).order_by(Member.display_name.asc())).scalars().all()
    70→    return [
    71→        MemberResponse(
    72→            id=row.id,
    73→            display_name=row.display_name,
    74→            ha_user_id=row.ha_user_id,
    75→            ha_person_entity_id=row.ha_person_entity_id,
    76→            notify_service=row.notify_service,
    77→            active=row.active,
    78→        )
    79→        for row in rows
    80→    ]
    81→
    82→
    83→@app.put("/v1/members/sync", response_model=MembersSyncResponse, dependencies=[Depends(require_token)])
    84→def put_members_sync(
    85→    payload: MembersSyncRequest,
    86→    session: Session = Depends(get_session),
    87→) -> MembersSyncResponse:
    88→    rows, deactivated_member_ids = sync_members(session, payload.members)
    89→    cleaning.sync_rotation_members(session)
    90→    inactive_member_ids = {
    91→        int(row.id)
    92→        for row in rows
    93→        if getattr(row, "id", None) is not None and not bool(getattr(row, "active", True))
    94→    }
    95→    notifications = cleaning.cancel_overrides_for_inactive_members(
    96→        session,
    97→        inactive_member_ids=inactive_member_ids,
    98→        actor_user_id=None,
    99→    )
   100→    return MembersSyncResponse(

1→"""Test fixtures for hass-flatmate service."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import os
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→
     8→import pytest
     9→from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
    10→
    11→
    12→@pytest.fixture
    13→def client(tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> TestClient:
    14→    db_path = tmp_path / "test.db"
    15→    monkeypatch.setenv("HASS_FLATMATE_DB_PATH", str(db_path))
    16→    monkeypatch.setenv("HASS_FLATMATE_API_TOKEN", "test-token")
    17→
    18→    from app import db
    19→    from app.db import Base
    20→    from app.main import app
    21→
    22→    db.configure_engine(f"sqlite:///{db_path}")
    23→    assert db.engine is not None
    24→    Base.metadata.drop_all(bind=db.engine)
    25→    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=db.engine)
    26→
    27→    with TestClient(app) as api_client:
    28→        yield api_client
    29→
    30→
    31→@pytest.fixture
    32→def auth_headers() -> dict[str, str]:
    33→    return {"x-flatmate-token": "test-token"}
    34→

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/with-contenv bashio
set -euo pipefail

API_TOKEN="$(bashio::config 'api_token')"
DB_PATH="/config/hass_flatmate_service/hass_flatmate.db"

export HASS_FLATMATE_API_TOKEN="${API_TOKEN}"
export HASS_FLATMATE_DB_PATH="${DB_PATH}"
export HASS_FLATMATE_HOST="0.0.0.0"
export HASS_FLATMATE_PORT="8099"

bashio::log.info "Starting hass-flatmate service"
python3 /opt/hass_flatmate/run.py

"""SQLAlchemy models for flatmate service."""

from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import date, datetime, timezone
from enum import Enum

from sqlalchemy import (
    JSON,
    Boolean,
    Date,
    DateTime,
    Enum as SAEnum,
    ForeignKey,
    Integer,
    String,
    Text,
    UniqueConstraint,
)
from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship

from .db import Base


def utc_now() -> datetime:
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)


class ShoppingStatus(str, Enum):
    OPEN = "open"

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report:

---

## HASS-FLATMATE Codebase Analysis

I've completed a thorough exploration of the hass-flatmate project. Here's a comprehensive overview to help you create an effective CLAUDE.md file:

### **1. Project Structure**

The project has three main components:

```
/Users/ms/Projects/private/hass-flatmate/
├── addon/hass_flatmate_service/              # Canonical backend source (FastAPI + SQLite)
│   ├── app/                                   # Main FastAPI application
│   │   ├── main.py                           # API endpoints, routes
│   │   ├── models.py                         # SQLAlchemy ORM models
│   │   ├── schemas.py                        # Pydantic request/response schemas
│   │   ├── db.py                             # Database configuration
│   │   ├── settings.py                       # Environment configuration
│   │   └── services/                         # Business logic
│   │       ├── cleaning.py                   # Cleaning rotation/swap logic
│   │       ├── shopping.py                   # Shopping list management
│   │       ├── activity.py                   # Activity event tracking
│   │       ├── members.py                    # Member synchronization
│   │       ├── importer.py                   # Manual data import
│   │       └── time_utils.py                 # Date/time utilities
│   ├── tests/                                # Test suite (pytest)
│   │   ├── conftest.py                       # pytest fixtures
│   │   ├── test_cleaning_api.py              # Cleaning tests
│   │   ├── test_shopping_api.py              # Shopping tests
│   │   ├── test_import_api.py                # Import tests
│   │   ├── test_settings.py                  # Settings tests
│   │   └── test_auth.py                      # Auth tests
│   ├── pyproject.toml                        # Python package config, test deps
│   └── run.py                                # Uvicorn entry point
│
├── apps/hass_flatmate_service/               # Home Assistant App packaging
│   ├── service_src/                          # Synced mirror of addon/ (for Docker build)
│   ├── config.yaml                           # App manifest (version, slug, ports)
│   ├── build.yaml                            # Docker base image config
│   ├── Dockerfile                            # Multi-arch build (amd64, aarch64)
│   ├── rootfs/run.sh                         # App startup script (bashio)
│   └── CHANGELOG.md                          # App-specific changelog
│
├── custom_components/hass_flatmate/          # Home Assistant integration (HACS)
│   ├── __init__.py                           # Setup, platforms, services
│   ├── manifest.json                         # Integration metadata
│   ├── api.py                                # API client to backend service
│   ├── coordinator.py                        # Coordinator for data sync
│   ├── config_flow.py                        # Configuration UI
│   ├── const.py                              # Constants, entity IDs
│   ├── services.yaml                         # Service definitions
│   ├── sensor.py                             # Sensor entities
│   ├── switch.py                             # Switch entities (test mode, etc)
│   ├── select.py                             # Select entities (member selection)
│   ├── text.py                               # Text entities (notification links)
│   ├── button.py                             # Button entities
│   ├── calendar.py                           # Calendar entity
│   ├── image.py                              # Image entity (SVG distribution)
│   ├── entity.py                             # Base entity class
│   ├── discovery.py                          # Service discovery via mDNS
│   ├── frontend/                             # Custom Lovelace cards (JavaScript)
│   │   ├── hass-flatmate-shopping-card.js           # Interactive shopping card
│   │   ├── hass-flatmate-shopping-compact-card.js   # Read-only compact shopping
│   │   ├── hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js           # Cleaning rotation card
│   │   └── hass-flatmate-distribution-card.js       # Shopping distribution card
│   └── translations/                         # i18n strings
│
├── .github/workflows/                        # GitHub Actions CI/CD
│   ├── ci.yml                                # Backend tests, frontend checks, app build test
│   └── publish-app.yml                       # Docker image publishing to GHCR
│
├── docs/
│   ├── ARCHITECTURE.md                       # Component design
│   ├── TESTING.md                            # Test setup guide
│   └── INSTALLATION.md
│
├── scripts/
│   └── sync_app_service_src.sh               # Sync addon/ → apps/service_src/
│
├── README.md                                 # Main project documentation
├── CHANGELOG.md                              # Repository changelog
├── Makefile                                  # Simple build targets
└── hacs.json                                 # HACS repository metadata
```

---

### **2. Technology Stack**

**Backend (FastAPI service):**
- Python 3.12+ (minimum version hard requirement)
- FastAPI 0.115+ (REST API framework)
- Uvicorn 0.30+ (ASGI server)
- SQLAlchemy 2.0+ (ORM)
- Pydantic 2.8+ (data validation)
- SQLite (persistent database)

**Frontend (Custom cards):**
- JavaScript/HTML (Lit-based Web Components)
- Shadow DOM (encapsulation)
- Home Assistant frontend APIs

**CI/CD:**
- GitHub Actions (workflows)
- Home Assistant builder (multi-arch Docker builds: amd64, aarch64)
- pytest (testing framework)
- Node.js (JavaScript syntax checks)

**Deployment:**
- Docker container (Home Assistant App)
- Home Assistant integration (custom component)
- GHCR (GitHub Container Registry) for images
- HACS for integration distribution

---

### **3. Build/Run/Test Commands**

**Backend Setup & Testing:**
```bash
cd addon/hass_flatmate_service
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e '.[test]'  # Install with test dependencies
pytest                     # Run full test suite
```

**Quick local test (from Makefile):**
```bash
make test
```

**App Source Sync (before building Docker image):**
```bash
./scripts/sync_app_service_src.sh
# or
make sync-app-source
```

**App Startup (Docker):**
```bash
# Environment variables set via rootfs/run.sh:
# HASS_FLATMATE_API_TOKEN (from app config)
# HASS_FLATMATE_DB_PATH (persists in /config/hass_flatmate_service/)
# HASS_FLATMATE_HOST (0.0.0.0)
# HASS_FLATMATE_PORT (8099)
python3 run.py
```

**Frontend JavaScript Validation:**
```bash
node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-shopping-card.js
node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js
node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-distribution-card.js
```

---

### **4. Architecture Overview**

**Data Flow:**

1. **Integration (HA)** → syncs users/persons via `members/sync` endpoint
2. **Integration** → polls backend every minute for notifications due (coordinator pattern)
3. **Backend** → dispatches notifications through HA notify services
4. **Integration** → hosts static frontend assets (custom cards)
5. **Custom Cards** → call integration services (shopping/cleaning actions)
6. **Services** → marshal calls to backend API (with token auth)

**Key Design Patterns:**

- **Service Separation:** Backend owns all domain state (members, shopping, cleaning, activity)
- **Integration owns:** polling coordinator, HA entity representations, service dispatch
- **Token Authentication:** `X-Flatmate-Token` header on all API requests
- **Database:** SQLite in `/config/hass_flatmate_service/` (persistent across restarts)
- **Activity Events:** All actions logged to activity table, emitted on HA event bus
- **Notification Flow:** Service returns notifications due for exact minute, integration dispatches via `notify.*`

---

### **5. Testing Setup**

**Test Infrastructure:**
- pytest with TestClient (FastAPI test client)
- Temporary SQLite database per test (tmp_path fixture)
- Mock environment variables (monkeypatch)
- Auth headers fixture with "test-token"
- Database auto-cleanup between tests

**Test Coverage:**
- Cleaning rotation, manual swaps, takeover completion
- Compensation override auto-planning and conflict handling
- Shopping lifecycle, stats, unknown actor exclusion
- Recents/favorites behavior
- SVG rendering endpoint
- Monday/Sunday notification schedule
- Member sync with deactivation

**CI Pipeline:**
- Backend tests (ubuntu-latest, Python 3.12)
- App source mirror sync validation
- Frontend card JavaScript syntax check (Node.js 22)
- Home Assistant app test build (home-assistant/builder@2025.11.0, amd64 + aarch64)

---

### **6. Configuration & Deployment**

**App Configuration (config.yaml):**
- Version: 0.1.21
- Port: 8099
- Arch: amd64, aarch64
- Mount: addon_config (read-write) → /config/hass_flatmate_service/
- Option: api_token (required, set by user)

**Integration Configuration (manifest.json):**
- Domain: hass_flatmate
- Version: 0.1.21
- Requirements: none (pure Python)
- HA minimum: 2025.1.0

**Docker Publishing (CI):**
- Trigger: git tag (v*), release published, or workflow_dispatch
- Registry: ghcr.io/gitviola/hass-flatmate-service-{arch}
- Version validation: tag version must match config.yaml version

---

### **7. Key File Purposes**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `app/main.py` | FastAPI app, all API endpoints, auth middleware |
| `app/models.py` | SQLAlchemy ORM models (Member, ShoppingItem, etc.) |
| `app/schemas.py` | Pydantic request/response models |
| `app/services/cleaning.py` | Rotation logic, swap/override management, notification scheduling |
| `app/services/shopping.py` | Shopping list CRUD, favorites, stats |
| `app/services/members.py` | Member sync from HA users/persons |
| `app/services/importer.py` | CSV-like bulk data import |
| `custom_components/hass_flatmate/__init__.py` | Setup platforms, register services, listeners |
| `custom_components/hass_flatmate/api.py` | HTTP client to backend service |
| `custom_components/hass_flatmate/coordinator.py` | Data polling and caching |
| `custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/*.js` | Interactive/read-only Lovelace cards |

---

### **8. Development Workflow**

**Typical Development Cycle:**

1. Make backend changes in `addon/hass_flatmate_service/app/`
2. Run local tests: `cd addon/hass_flatmate_service && pytest`
3. If tests pass, sync app build source: `./scripts/sync_app_service_src.sh`
4. Build/deploy app in Home Assistant only after tests pass
5. Commit changes with clear message (push to feature branch)
6. PR review → merge to main
7. Tag release (e.g., `v0.1.22`) to trigger GHCR image publishing

**GitHub Actions:**
- CI runs on every push (tests, checks)
- Publish workflow triggered by tag push or manual dispatch
- Both workflows must pass before merge to main

---

### **9. Key Environment Variables**

| Variable | Default | Usage |
|----------|---------|-------|
| `HASS_FLATMATE_API_TOKEN` | "dev-token" | Service API authentication |
| `HASS_FLATMATE_DB_PATH` | `/config/hass_flatmate_service/hass_flatmate.db` (HA) or `./data/hass_flatmate.db` (dev) | SQLite database location |
| `HASS_FLATMATE_HOST` | 0.0.0.0 | Bind address |
| `HASS_FLATMATE_PORT` | 8099 | Service port |

---

### **10. Important Notes**

- **No linting/formatting tools:** No .eslintrc, prettier, black, isort, mypy, ruff configs found
- **No pre-commit hooks:** No .pre-commit-config.yaml
- **Canonical source is addon/:** Always edit `addon/hass_flatmate_service/`, then sync to `apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src/`
- **Version sync requirement:** Both app config.yaml and integration manifest.json must have matching version for releases
- **Python 3.12 minimum:** Hard requirement in pyproject.toml
- **Database persistence:** Stored in HA supervisor mount, survives restarts/updates, included in HA backups
- **Weekly cleanup:** Tests use temporary databases, no manual DB management needed

---

This comprehensive analysis should provide the foundation for creating an effective CLAUDE.md file tailored to this project's workflows and conventions.

### **8. Development Workflow**

**Typical Development Cycle:**

1. Make backend changes in `addon/hass_flatmate_service/app/`
2. Run local tests: `cd addon/hass_flatmate_service && pytest`
3. If tests pass, sync app build source: `./scripts/sync_app_service_src.sh`
4. Build/deploy app in Home Assistant only after tests pass
5. Commit changes with clear message (push to feature branch)
6. PR review → merge to main
7. Tag release (e.g., `v0.1.22`) to trigger GHCR image publishing

**GitHub Actions:**
- CI runs on every push (tests, checks)
- Publish workflow triggered by tag push or manual dispatch
- Both workflows must pass before merge to main

---

### **9. Key Environment Variables**

| Variable | Default | Usage |
|----------|---------|-------|
| `HASS_FLATMATE_API_TOKEN` | "dev-token" | Service API authentication |
| `HASS_FLATMATE_DB_PATH` | `/config/hass_flatmate_service/hass_flatmate.db` (HA) or `./data/hass_flatmate.db` (dev) | SQLite database location |
| `HASS_FLATMATE_HOST` | 0.0.0.0 | Bind address |
| `HASS_FLATMATE_PORT` | 8099 | Service port |

---

### **10. Important Notes**

- **No linting/formatting tools:** No .eslintrc, prettier, black, isort, mypy, ruff configs found
- **No pre-commit hooks:** No .pre-commit-config.yaml
- **Canonical source is addon/:** Always edit `addon/hass_flatmate_service/`, then sync to `apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src/`
- **Version sync requirement:** Both app config.yaml and integration manifest.json must have matching version for releases
- **Python 3.12 minimum:** Hard requirement in pyproject.toml
- **Database persistence:** Stored in HA supervisor mount, survives restarts/updates, included in HA backups
- **Weekly cleanup:** Tests use temporary databases, no manual DB management needed

---

This comprehensive analysis should provide the foundation for creating an effective CLAUDE.md file tailored to this project's workflows and conventions.
Now let me check the README and any existing docs for important details.

file.content:
# Hass Flatmate

Home Assistant-native flat-sharing toolkit with two parts:

- `custom_components/hass_flatmate`: HACS custom integration
- `apps/hass_flatmate_service`: Home Assistant App (formerly add-on) backend service

It replaces the flat-sharing features you use with:

- Shopping list actions (add, complete, delete)
- Fairness distribution (90-day) + SVG chart image
- Weekly cleaning rotation with temporary swaps
- Takeover completion with automatic compensation override
- Home Assistant calendar activity events
- Built-in notification scheduling (Monday + Sunday reminders)
- Optional shopping-added push notifications
- Notification deep links (iOS + Android)
- Automation-friendly activity events on HA event bus
- Safe notification test mode (redirect all notifications to one selected user)

## One-Click Install

### Home Assistant App (backend)

[![Add app repository to your Home Assistant instance.](https://my.home-assistant.io/badges/supervisor_add_addon_repository.svg)](https://my.home-assistant.io/redirect/supervisor_add_addon_repository/?repository_url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fgitviola%2Fhass-flatmate)
[![Show app on your Home Assistant instance.](https://my.home-assistant.io/badges/supervisor_addon.svg)](https://my.home-assistant.io/redirect/supervisor_addon/?addon=hass_flatmate_service&repository_url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fgitviola%2Fhass-flatmate)

### HACS integration

[![Open HACS repository on your Home Assistant instance.](https://my.home-assistant.io/badges/hacs_repository.svg)](https://my.home-assistant.io/redirect/hacs_repository/?owner=gitviola&repository=hass-flatmate&category=integration)

## Repository Layout

- `/custom_components/hass_flatmate`: install via HACS (custom repository)
- `/apps/hass_flatmate_service`: install via Home Assistant App repository
- `/addon/hass_flatmate_service`: canonical backend source + tests
- `/apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src`: app build source mirror (synced from `addon/`)

## Quick Start

1. Install app from this repository (`apps/hass_flatmate_service`) in Home Assistant.
2. Set app option `api_token`.
   - Persistent DB path: `/config/hass_flatmate_service/hass_flatmate.db` (Supervisor `addon_config` mount).
   - Data survives container restarts/image updates; include app/add-on data in HA backups for disaster recovery.
3. Install custom integration via HACS from this repository.
4. Configure integration with:
   - `base_url`: service URL (default `http://ebc95cb1-hass-flatmate-service:8099`)
   - `api_token`: same value from app config
5. In Lovelace storage mode, shopping and cleaning card resources are auto-registered with cache-busting version query params.
6. Add cards/entities from `examples/lovelace-flatmate-dashboard.yaml` (Sections dashboard format).

## Shopping UI Card

Use the custom card in any dashboard:

```yaml
type: custom:hass-flatmate-shopping-card
entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_shopping_data
title: Shared Shopping
```

You can also add it from the dashboard UI card picker (no manual YAML required).

Features:
- Add shopping items fast
- Mark item done / delete item
- Quick add from favorites and recents
- Add/remove favorites

The card uses integration services, so actor attribution stays correct.

## Shopping Compact UI Card

Use the dedicated read-only compact card for e-ink/non-touch displays:

```yaml
type: custom:hass-flatmate-shopping-compact-card
entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_shopping_data
title: Shopping List
```

Features:
- Dense, bullet-free list layout
- Relative age labels like `added 5 hours ago` / `added 2 weeks ago`
- Read-only rendering optimized for passive displays
- Available in the card picker (no manual YAML required)

## Cleaning UI Card

Use the dedicated cleaning card in any dashboard:

```yaml
type: custom:hass-flatmate-cleaning-card
entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_cleaning_schedule
title: Weekly Cleaning
weeks: 5
layout: interactive
```

Features:
- Human-friendly week list (`This week`, `Next week`, etc.)
- Clear done/pending/missed status
- Strike-through visual once completed
- Mark-done action in-card
- Non-assignee completion modal (confirm for assignee vs takeover)
- Click any week row (interactive layout) to open 7-day shift history and notification delivery status
- Optional `layout: compact` read-only mode for e-ink/non-touch dashboards
- Compact mode still shows swap/compensation annotations and who is assigned

Manual swap override remains available via service:
- `hass_flatmate.hass_flatmate_swap_cleaning_week`

In Lovelace YAML resource mode, add both resources manually as `module` (recommended with `?v=<integration_version>`):
- `/hass_flatmate/static/hass-flatmate-shopping-card.js?v=<integration_version>`
- `/hass_flatmate/static/hass-flatmate-shopping-compact-card.js?v=<integration_version>`
- `/hass_flatmate/static/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js?v=<integration_version>`

Compact e-ink example:

```yaml
type: custom:hass-flatmate-cleaning-card
entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_cleaning_schedule
title: Cleaning (E-Ink)
weeks: 5
layout: compact
```

## Distribution UI Card

Use the dedicated distribution card instead of the SVG image entity card:

```yaml
type: custom:hass-flatmate-distribution-card
entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_shopping_distribution_90d
title: Shopping Distribution (90d)
layout: bars
```

Features:
- Stable native rendering in two styles:
  - `layout: bars` (default)
  - `layout: compact` (single-row boxes, e-ink friendly)
- Shows all flatmates from distribution payload (including zero-count members)
- Uses purchase wording (`N purchases`) and last-90-days context text

In Lovelace YAML resource mode, also add:
- `/hass_flatmate/static/hass-flatmate-distribution-card.js?v=<integration_version>`

Compact example:

```yaml
type: custom:hass-flatmate-distribution-card
entity: sensor.hass_flatmate_shopping_distribution_90d
title: Shopping Distribution (Compact)
layout: compact
```

## Notification Test Mode

Use these entities to test all flows without notifying everyone:
- `switch.hass_flatmate_notification_test_mode`
- `select.hass_flatmate_notification_test_target`
- `switch.hass_flatmate_notify_shopping_item_added`
- `text.hass_flatmate_shopping_notification_link`
- `text.hass_flatmate_cleaning_notification_link`
- `select.hass_flatmate_shopping_calendar_target`
- `select.hass_flatmate_cleaning_calendar_target`

When test mode is enabled, all notifications are redirected to the selected target and prefixed with `[TEST]`.

`*_notification_link` accepts:
- Relative dashboard/view path like `/dashboard-flatmate/shopping`
- Full URL like `https://...`
- Companion deep link like `homeassistant://navigate/dashboard-flatmate/shopping`

The integration attaches this link to mobile notifications using both iOS `url` and Android `clickAction`.

## Automation Event Triggers

The integration emits Home Assistant bus events for every new activity row:
- Generic: `hass_flatmate_activity`
- Action-specific: `hass_flatmate_activity_<action>`
  - Example: `hass_flatmate_activity_shopping_item_added`
  - Example: `hass_flatmate_activity_cleaning_done`

Event payload includes:
- `activity_id`, `domain`, `action`, `created_at`
- `actor_member_id`, `actor_name`, `actor_user_id_raw`
- `payload` (raw activity payload JSON), `summary`

This enables per-user custom automations/notifications without changing integration code.

## Manual Data Import

Use service `hass_flatmate_import_manual_data` from Developer Tools to paste CSV-like rows:

- `rotation_rows` format:
  - `date,member_name`
  - date can be any date inside that week (`YYYY-MM-DD` or ISO datetime)
  - rows can be newline or `;` separated
- `cleaning_history_rows` format:
  - `date,member_name[,status][,completed_by_name]`
  - status: `done` (default), `missed`, `pending`
- `shopping_history_rows` format:
  - `date,item_name,buyer_name`
- `cleaning_override_rows` format:
  - `date,member_from_name,member_to_name[,override_type]`
  - `override_type`: `compensation` (default) or `manual_swap`
  - For compensation, `member_from_name` is the person whose baseline turn is being covered.

Example:

```yaml
service: hass_flatmate.hass_flatmate_import_manual_data
data:
  rotation_rows: |
    2026-02-14,Martin
    2026-02-21,Gianmarco
    2026-02-28,María
  cleaning_history_rows: |
    2026-02-01,Martin,done
    2026-02-08,Gianmarco,done
  shopping_history_rows: |
    2026-01-30,Milk,Martin
    2026-01-31,Toilet Paper,Gianmarco
  cleaning_override_rows: |
    2026-03-07,Martin,Martina,compensation
```

Notes:
- Dates can be any day in the target week. Import normalizes to Monday-week internally.
- Member names must match active hass-flatmate display names exactly (including accents).

## Automated Tests

Backend tests run locally without Home Assistant:

```bash
cd addon/hass_flatmate_service
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e '.[test]'
pytest
```

CI also runs on every push/PR:
- Backend test suite
- Integration compile check
- Shopping/cleaning custom card JavaScript syntax check
- Home Assistant app test build (via `home-assistant/builder`)
- Sync guard for `apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src`

## Image Publishing (GHCR)

App images are published automatically to GHCR by GitHub Actions:
- Workflow: `.github/workflows/publish-app.yml`
- Trigger: git tag push like `v0.1.1` (also supports Release `published` and manual `workflow_dispatch`)
- Registry image: `ghcr.io/gitviola/hass-flatmate-service-{arch}`
- Architectures: `amd64`, `aarch64`

Release/tag version must match `apps/hass_flatmate_service/config.yaml` `version`.

## Changelogs

- Repo changelog: `/CHANGELOG.md`
- Integration changelog: `/custom_components/hass_flatmate/CHANGELOG.md`
- App changelog: `/apps/hass_flatmate_service/CHANGELOG.md`

## Syncing App Build Source

When backend code changes in `addon/hass_flatmate_service`, sync mirror source used by app Docker build:

```bash
./scripts/sync_app_service_src.sh
```

     2→

     4→	cd addon/hass_flatmate_service && . .venv/bin/activate && pytest
     5→
     6→sync-app-source:
     7→	./scripts/sync_app_service_src.sh
     8→

	cd addon/hass_flatmate_service && . .venv/bin/activate && pytest

sync-app-source:

[project]
name = "hass-flatmate-service"
version = "0.1.20"
description = "Flatmate shopping and cleaning service for Home Assistant"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
  "fastapi>=0.115.0,<1.0.0",
  "uvicorn>=0.30.0,<1.0.0",
  "sqlalchemy>=2.0.30,<3.0.0",
  "pydantic>=2.8.0,<3.0.0",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
test = [
  "pytest>=8.0.0,<9.0.0",
  "httpx>=0.27.0,<1.0.0",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=68", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
addopts = "-q"
testpaths = ["tests"]

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
  pull_request:

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  backend-tests:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install backend test deps
        run: |
          cd addon/hass_flatmate_service
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -e '.[test]'

      - name: Run backend tests
        run: |
          cd addon/hass_flatmate_service
          pytest

      - name: Compile custom integration
        run: |
          python -m compileall custom_components/hass_flatmate

  sync-app-source-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Validate app source mirror sync
        run: |
          ./scripts/sync_app_service_src.sh
          git diff --exit-code -- apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src

  frontend-card-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: "22"

      - name: Validate shopping and cleaning card syntax
        run: |
          node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-shopping-card.js
          node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js
          node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-distribution-card.js

  app-build-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Test Home Assistant app build
        uses: home-assistant/builder@2025.11.0
        with:
          args: |
            --test \
            --amd64 \
            --aarch64 \
            --target ./apps/hass_flatmate_service \
            --image ghcr.io/gitviola/hass-flatmate-service-{arch}

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"

rsync -a --delete \
  --exclude '.venv' \
  --exclude '__pycache__' \
  --exclude '.pytest_cache' \
  "${ROOT_DIR}/addon/hass_flatmate_service/" \
  "${ROOT_DIR}/apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src/"

echo "Synced addon/hass_flatmate_service -> apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src"

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Home Assistant flat-sharing toolkit with three components:
- **`addon/hass_flatmate_service/`** — Canonical backend source (FastAPI + SQLite). All backend edits go here.
- **`apps/hass_flatmate_service/`** — HA App packaging (Dockerfile, config). `service_src/` is a mirror of `addon/` for Docker builds.
- **`custom_components/hass_flatmate/`** — HA custom integration (HACS): entities, services, coordinator, frontend cards.

## Commands

```bash
# Run backend tests (primary development loop)
cd addon/hass_flatmate_service && pytest

# Or via Makefile from repo root
make test

# Run a single test
cd addon/hass_flatmate_service && pytest tests/test_cleaning_api.py::test_function_name

# Install test dependencies (first time / after pyproject.toml changes)
cd addon/hass_flatmate_service && pip install -e '.[test]'

# Sync backend source to app build mirror (required after backend changes, CI enforces this)
./scripts/sync_app_service_src.sh
# or: make sync-app-source

# Validate frontend card JavaScript syntax
node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-shopping-card.js
node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js
node --check custom_components/hass_flatmate/frontend/hass-flatmate-distribution-card.js

# Compile-check the integration Python
python -m compileall custom_components/hass_flatmate
```

## Architecture

### Data flow

Integration (HA) syncs persons to backend via `POST /members/sync` → Backend owns all domain state (shopping, cleaning, activity) in SQLite → Integration polls backend every 60s via coordinator → Backend returns notifications due for the current minute → Integration dispatches via HA `notify.*` services → Frontend cards call integration services → Integration marshals to backend API.

### Backend (FastAPI)

- `app/main.py` — All API routes + token auth middleware (`X-Flatmate-Token` header)
- `app/models.py` — SQLAlchemy ORM models
- `app/schemas.py` — Pydantic request/response schemas
- `app/services/cleaning.py` — Rotation logic, swap/override management, notification scheduling
- `app/services/shopping.py` — Shopping list CRUD, favorites, stats, distribution
- `app/services/members.py` — Member sync from HA persons
- `app/settings.py` — Env-based config (`HASS_FLATMATE_API_TOKEN`, `HASS_FLATMATE_DB_PATH`, etc.)

### Integration (custom component)

- `__init__.py` — Platform setup, service registration, event bus listeners
- `api.py` — HTTP client to backend
- `coordinator.py` — Polling data coordinator
- `sensor.py` — Sensor entities (shopping data, cleaning schedule, distribution)
- `frontend/*.js` — Lit-based custom Lovelace cards (shadow DOM)

### Testing

- pytest with FastAPI TestClient, temporary SQLite DB per test (`tmp_path` fixture)
- Auth via `X-Flatmate-Token: test-token` header fixture
- No mocking frameworks — tests exercise the full API stack

## Key Development Rules

- **Always edit `addon/hass_flatmate_service/`**, never `apps/.../service_src/` directly. Run `sync_app_service_src.sh` after backend changes.
- CI enforces the sync: `git diff --exit-code -- apps/hass_flatmate_service/service_src` must be clean.
- Version must match in both `apps/hass_flatmate_service/config.yaml` and `custom_components/hass_flatmate/manifest.json` for releases.
- Python 3.12+ required. No linter/formatter configured — follow existing code style.
- Frontend cards are vanilla JS (Lit patterns, shadow DOM). No build step; validate with `node --check`.
- Entity IDs use domain prefix `hass_flatmate_` (e.g., `sensor.hass_flatmate_shopping_data`).

+

+        "skipped",

+    }
+    inserted = 0
+

+                "status must be one of: sent, failed, skipped, suppressed, test_redirected"
+            )
+
+        member_id_raw = record.get("member_id")
+        member_id = None
+        if member_id_raw is not None:
+            try:
+                member_id = int(member_id_raw)
+            except (TypeError, ValueError):
+                member_id = None
+
+        dispatched_at = record.get("dispatched_at")
+        created_at = dispatched_at if isinstance(dispatched_at, datetime) else None
+
+        log_event(
+            session,
+            domain="cleaning",
+            action="cleaning_notification_dispatch",
+            actor_member_id=None,
+            actor_user_id_raw=None,
+            payload={
+                "week_start": week_start_raw.isoformat(),
+                "member_id": member_id,
+                "notify_service": str(record.get("notify_service") or "") or None,
+                "title": str(record.get("title") or "") or None,
+                "message": str(record.get("message") or "") or None,
+                "notification_kind": str(record.get("notification_kind") or "") or None,
+                "notification_slot": str(record.get("notification_slot") or "") or None,
+                "source_action": str(record.get("source_action") or "") or None,
+                "status": status_raw,
+                "reason": str(record.get("reason") or "") or None,
+            },
+            created_at=created_at,
+        )
+        inserted += 1
+
+    session.commit()
+    return inserted

diff --git c/custom_components/hass_flatmate/__init__.py w/custom_components/hass_flatmate/__init__.py

@@ -244,6 +244,47 @@ def _notification_data_payload(*, category: str | None, link: str | None) -> dic
     return data
 
 
+def _coerce_week_start(value: Any) -> str | None:
+    if isinstance(value, date):
+        return value.isoformat()
+    if not isinstance(value, str):
+        return None
+    normalized = value.strip().split("T", maxsplit=1)[0]
+    if not normalized:
+        return None
+    try:
+        return date.fromisoformat(normalized).isoformat()
+    except ValueError:
+        return None
+
+
+def _build_cleaning_dispatch_record(
+    item: dict[str, Any],
+    *,
+    notify_service: str | None,
+    status: str,
+    reason: str | None = None,
+) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
+    week_start = _coerce_week_start(item.get("week_start"))
+    if week_start is None:
+        return None
+
+    member_id = _coerce_member_id(item.get("member_id"))
+    return {
+        "week_start": week_start,
+        "member_id": member_id,
+        "notify_service": notify_service,
+        "title": item.get("title"),
+        "message": item.get("message"),
+        "notification_kind": item.get("notification_kind"),
+        "notification_slot": item.get("notification_slot"),
+        "source_action": item.get("source_action"),
+        "status": status,
+        "reason": reason,
+        "dispatched_at": dt_util.utcnow().isoformat(),
+    }
+
+

+

         _LOGGER.warning(
             "Notification test mode is enabled but no valid target member is configured; "
             "suppressing %s notifications",
             len(notifications),
         )
+        for item in notifications:
+            category = item.get("category") if isinstance(item.get("category"), str) else default_category
+            category = str(category) if category else None
+            if category != "cleaning":
+                continue
+            record = _build_cleaning_dispatch_record(
+                item,
+                notify_service=item.get("notify_service"),

+    if action == "cleaning_compensation_planned":
+        return f"{actor_name} planned a make-up shift"
+    if action == "cleaning_override_auto_canceled_member_inactive":
+        return "A planned override was canceled because a flatmate left"
+    if action == "cleaning_notification_dispatch":
+        status = str(payload.get("status", "")).strip().lower()

+        slot_label_map = {

+            "sunday_21": "Sun 21:00 final reminder",

+
+def _build_cleaning_history_by_week(

+    cutoff = now_local - timedelta(days=7)
+    history_by_week: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = {}
+
+    for row in activity_rows:
+        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
+            continue
+
+        if str(row.get("domain", "")).strip().lower() != "cleaning":
+            continue
+
+        payload = row.get("payload_json", {})
+        if not isinstance(payload, Mapping):

+            continue
+
+        action = str(row.get("action", "")).strip().lower()

+        history_item = {
+            "id": row.get("id"),
+            "created_at": created_local.isoformat(),
+            "summary": _cleaning_history_summary(action, payload, members, actor_name),
+            "action": action,
+            "actor_name": actor_name,
+            "notification_slot": payload.get("notification_slot"),
+            "dispatch_status": payload.get("status"),
+            "reason": payload.get("reason"),
+            "_sort_key": created_local.timestamp(),
+        }
+
+        for week_start in week_starts:
+            history_by_week.setdefault(week_start, []).append(dict(history_item))
+
+    for week_start, events in history_by_week.items():
+        events.sort(key=lambda item: float(item.get("_sort_key", 0.0)), reverse=True)
+        trimmed = events[:30]

+    return history_by_week

+
+def _notification_slots_for_week(
+    *,

+        "skipped": "Skipped",
+        "suppressed": "Suppressed",
+        "pending": "Pending",
+        "missing": "Missing",
+        "not_required": "Not required",
+    }
+
+    latest_dispatch_by_slot: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
+    for event in history:
+        if str(event.get("action", "")).strip().lower() != "cleaning_notification_dispatch":
+            continue
+        slot = str(event.get("notification_slot", "")).strip().lower()
+        if not slot or slot in latest_dispatch_by_slot:
+            continue
+        latest_dispatch_by_slot[slot] = event
+
+    slots: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+    for slot_id, label, day_offset, hour in slot_defs:
+        slot_moment = datetime.combine(

+            tzinfo=slot_tz,
+        )
+
+        dispatch_event = latest_dispatch_by_slot.get(slot_id)
+        if dispatch_event is not None:

+            detail = dispatch_event.get("summary")
+            slots.append(

+                    "slot": slot_id,

+                    "state": state,
+                    "state_label": status_labels.get(state, state.title()),
+                    "detail": detail,
+                    "last_event_at": dispatch_event.get("created_at"),
+                }
+            )
+            continue
+
+        if now_local < slot_moment:
+            state = "pending"

+            state = "not_required"
+        else:
+            state = "missing"
+
+        slots.append(
+            {
+                "slot": slot_id,
+                "label": label,
+                "state": state,
+                "state_label": status_labels.get(state, state.title()),
+                "detail": None,
+                "last_event_at": None,
+            }
+        )
+
+    return slots
+

     payload = row.get("payload_json", {})

+        if now_local < slot_moment:
+            state = "pending"
+        elif slot_id.startswith("sunday_") and completed_at is not None and completed_at <= slot_moment:

+        else:

+

+                "state_label": status_labels.get(state, state.title()),
+                "detail": None,
+                "last_event_at": None,
+            }
+        )
+
+    return slots

content.text:
Here's the full picture:

**The feature is fully implemented but not committed or released.** Here's what was built across ~1200 lines of uncommitted changes:

### What's done (all layers complete)

1. **Backend** (`addon/` + synced to `apps/service_src/`):
   - New `POST /v1/cleaning/notifications/dispatch` endpoint to record notification delivery outcomes
   - All notifications now carry `category`, `week_start`, `notification_kind`, `notification_slot`, and `source_action` metadata
   - `completed_at` timestamp added to schedule rows
   - `record_notification_dispatches()` persists dispatch events to the activity log
   - 2 new tests + enhanced existing tests — all 29 pass

2. **Integration** (`custom_components/`):
   - `_dispatch_notifications` now logs every cleaning notification outcome (sent/failed/skipped/suppressed/test_redirected) back to the backend
   - `api.py` has the new `record_cleaning_notification_dispatch()` client method
   - `sensor.py` builds per-week history from activity events + computes notification slot states (Mon 11:00 assignment, Sun 18:00 reminder, Sun 21:00 final reminder) with logic for pending/missing/not_required

3. **Frontend card** (`hass-flatmate-cleaning-card.js`):
   - Clicking any week row opens a "Shift history" modal
   - Modal shows assigned flatmate, notification delivery status (per slot with color-coded badges), and recent events (last 7 days)
   - Full styling, keyboard accessibility, backdrop close

4. **README** updated with the new feature bullet point

5. **App source sync** is clean (CI check would pass)

### What's NOT done

- **No commit** — everything is still unstaged working tree changes
- **No version bump** — still at 0.1.21 (same as the last tag `v0.1.21`)
- **No tag/release** — no new tag was pushed, so no new Docker image was published

file.content:
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project are documented in this file.

## [Unreleased]

### Changed
- Backend DB path resolution now defaults to `/config/hass_flatmate_service/hass_flatmate.db` when running inside Home Assistant, ensuring persistent storage by default.

### Added
- Backend tests for DB-path fallback behavior to guard persistent-storage defaults in Home Assistant and local-dev fallback behavior.

### Fixed
- Lovelace storage-mode card resources are now auto-registered with cache-busting version query params, and stale prior-version resource entries are auto-updated to reduce frontend JS caching issues after updates.

## [0.1.21] - 2026-02-12

### Changed
- Cleaning swap semantics now exchange two shifts (selected week plus the selected flatmate's next regular baseline week) instead of only replacing one week.
- Cleaning swap modal copy now explains the two-week exchange explicitly, including return-week preview.
- Cleaning modal week labels now include relative timing hints (for example `in 2 weeks`) to make outcome previews easier to understand.

### Fixed
- Cleaning schedule payload now includes `override_source` so UI can distinguish swap-return weeks from takeover make-up shifts.
- Cleaning schedule payload now includes `source_week_start` for linked compensation weeks, so UI can render concise context like `Swapped with Martina 3 weeks ago`.

## [0.1.20] - 2026-02-12

### Changed
- Compact cleaning card now prioritizes assignee-first readability for e-ink: assignee name is shown first in bold, with date/details on following lines.

# Hass Flatmate Service App Changelog

## [Unreleased]

### Changed
- Service DB-path fallback now defaults to `/config/hass_flatmate_service/hass_flatmate.db` whenever the Home Assistant `/config` mount is available, making persistence the safe default.

### Added
- Tests for DB-path resolution priority (explicit env override, HA persistent path fallback, local-dev fallback).

## [0.1.21] - 2026-02-12

### Changed
- `POST /v1/cleaning/overrides/swap` now applies a true two-week shift exchange by adding a linked return-week override automatically.
- Swap cancel now removes both linked weeks in the exchange and restores baseline assignment for both.

### Fixed
- `GET /v1/cleaning/schedule` now includes `source_week_start` for linked compensation rows so clients can show clear swap-return timing context.

## [0.1.20] - 2026-02-12

### Changed
- Swap and make-up-shift push notifications now include actor-aware context and clearer one-time action wording.

## [0.1.19] - 2026-02-12

### Changed
- Compensation notification text now uses clearer "make-up shift" wording.

## [0.1.15] - 2026-02-11

# Hass Flatmate Integration Changelog

## [Unreleased]

### Fixed
- Lovelace storage-mode frontend resources are now registered with cache-busting version query params and stale older-url resource entries are upgraded automatically to avoid card JS cache issues after updates.

## [0.1.21] - 2026-02-12

### Changed
- Cleaning card swap UX now describes and previews a two-week shift exchange (selected week + return week) rather than a single-week replacement.
- Cleaning done/swap modal week labels now include relative timing hints (for example `in 2 weeks`) for clearer consequence previews.

### Fixed
- Cleaning schedule sensor now exposes `override_source` and labels manual compensation rows as swap-return weeks for clearer UI wording.
- Cleaning schedule sensor now exposes `source_week_start`, enabling concise swap-return notes like `Swapped with Martina 3 weeks ago`.

## [0.1.20] - 2026-02-12

### Changed
- Compact cleaning card layout now shows assignee name first and in stronger bold to improve glanceability on e-ink displays.
- Cleaning swap modal title/choices now explicitly say "one-time swap" to avoid recurring-swap ambiguity.

### Fixed
- Compact cleaning card week-context labels (`this week`, `previous week`, `next week`) now render in italic styling.
- Compact cleaning card note lines (for example `Done by ...`) now render in italic styling.
- Cleaning modal preview now details per-person notification outcomes with actor-aware wording.
- Shopping add-item input now remains stable while typing during frequent state updates (focus no longer drops unexpectedly).

## [0.1.19] - 2026-02-12
